creator: brnrd@freebsd.org
text:
With CFLAGS set in /etc/make.conf to
     CFLAGS= -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
I get the following CFLAGS assignments in make -dv output
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-I${LOCALBASE}/include/event2/compat -I${LOCALBASE}/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe -march=pentium-m
-I/usr/local/include/event2/compat -I/usr/local/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-I${LOCALBASE}/include/event2/compat -I${LOCALBASE}/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe -march=pentium-m
-I/usr/local/include/event2/compat -I/usr/local/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-DBUILD="\"$(VERSION)\""

If I set CFLAGS to
CFLAGS+= -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
I get
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-I${LOCALBASE}/include/event2/compat -I${LOCALBASE}/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
-march=pentium-m -I/usr/local/include/event2/compat
-I/usr/local/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-I${LOCALBASE}/include/event2/compat -I${LOCALBASE}/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
-march=pentium-m -I/usr/local/include/event2/compat
-I/usr/local/include
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
-march=pentium-m -I/usr/local/include/event2/compat
-I/usr/local/include -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
-march=pentium-m -I/usr/local/include/event2/compat
-I/usr/local/include -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
Global:CFLAGS = -O2 -pipe -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
-march=pentium-m -I/usr/local/include/event2/compat
-I/usr/local/include -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe ${_CPUCFLAGS}
-DBUILD="\"$(VERSION)\""

Somewhere the CFLAGS assignments of tmux-1.4_5 are overwritten by make.conf's

Hope this helps, Spil
